title: lithocarpus psammophilus
intro: Lithocarpus psammophilus, also known as the sandstone copper fir or sandpaper tree, is a species of plant that belongs to the beech family, Fagaceae. This plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which are derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits associated with Lithocarpus psammophilus include the potential treatment of various diseases such as diabetes, cancer, and cardiovascular disorders, as well as its use as an antimicrobial agent. Lithocarpus psammophilus can be prepared into herbal remedies through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is traditionally used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
